reviews.review_msg:
*314

Super nice guy. I had him as my TA. I don't struggle with accents like my peers, so attending lectures / tutorial was not an issue. Generally, his lectures were helpful for picking out which questions he might test you on from his list of questions. Would highly recommend.

*314:Super nice guy, always looking out for everyone. His accent does make it hard to understand his lectures but his lecture slides do a good job covering everything. The tests consists of questions he provides from a “comprehensive list” of questions before the midterm and end of term test, which is super helpful. Overall I highly recommend him.

*CRIM 314* Not bad of a professor. He has a very strong accent, so it's hard to understand the lectures. The content was boring, but he's a funny professor which helps. The best thing about this course was that he gives you the exam questions, so studying for his exams was really easy. The TA was wonderful and helped me get through the course

Professor is a funny guy. Lectures are really boring and his accent makes it hard to understand him. However, he gives out the exam questions prior to the exam, which makes studying so much easier. Paper had broad guidelines. If he didn't give the exam questions beforehand, I probably would've failed. Also the TA for this course was awesome!

For 314* I don't understand the hate. Yeah his accent makes it hard to understand him but he literally gives all the exam questions a week before the exam. He does give out a long list of potential questions (was around 70) but what do you expect, this is a 3rd year course. Would definitely take his class again. He also gives off cute grandpa vibes

*CRIM 314* worst class I've taken in my 6 years at SFU. Genuinely. He was completely lost in his own course. Impossible to reach. Doesn't acts like he cares. Definitely uses AI for his slides, and if not, they're large excerpts from books written in the 1800s and talks about random stuff, not what's on screen. I could go on forever. Bless the TA.

FOR CRIM 314** With upmost respect, I could not understand his lectures. His accent is kind of tough and he has pictures on his slides that he rambles about for 2 hours. Exams he provides questions for but there are 75 and he chooses like 11 for the exams. Content is super dry and very confusing as articles came from the early 1900's. Not relevant.

*Crim314 Dr. Shchitov is a fair prof, who has the standard midterm, final, paper, & participation. The slides are thorough that go over the exam questions. However, to be successful in this class, you must keep up with the weekly discussion questions as they are necessary for both participation and exams. Highly recommend Dr. Shchitov.

*Crim314
Dr Shchitov gets a bit off-topic during lectures it's fun but his slides are barebones/vague at times so you really need to read.
I like the variety in reading/videos but the fact they're low resolution scans are a bit annoying.
We get test questions ahead of time, & marks are relatively generous.
It's a bit hard to really participate.

Really interesting guy. This class is not easy, and the textbook is hard to read at times, so you have to read ahead of lecture. Don't try and write everything down from the slides, instead focus on the extra material he talks about that isn't in his lecture material. Has pretty crazy stories about friends back in Russia.

Dr.S is awesome. Sometimes it might be hard to understand what he's saying, but need not worry! This class has 3 tests and a paper. For all 3 tests he posts a big list of study questions the week before the testand all the questions that will appear on the tests come from that list. He really wants his students to do well!
